steps.title: Find the true subject
steps.content: The sentence begins with an inverted structure: "So shrewd an observer is Shonagon... that her book's musings on tenth-century Japanese courtly life ______." Strip the prepositional phrase: the subject is "musings" (plural), not "book" or "life."

steps.title: Match verb to subject
steps.content: Rule: a plural subject requires a plural present-tense verb. "Musings" is plural, so the verb must be plural too. A. "fascinate" — plural present tense. Matches the plural subject "musings." Correct. B. "has fascinated" — singular present perfect ("has" marks singular). Fails: "musings has" violates agreement. C. "is fascinating" — singular present progressive. Fails: "musings is" violates agreement. D. "fascinates" — singular present tense (the -s ending marks singular). Fails: "musings fascinates" violates agreement. That matches choice A — A is correct.
